original_image_tags: A document stamp in the bottom left corner reads "COMMUNICATIONS SECTION MAILED 9 APR 27 1949 P.M. FEDERAL BUREAU OF INVESTIGATION U. S. DEPARTMENT OF JUSTICE".
A document stamp in the bottom right corner reads "RECEIVED READING ROOM APR 27 5 49 PM '49 FBI U.S. DEPT. OF JUSTICE DIRECTOR".
A document stamp in the bottom right corner reads "G.I.R.-7 APR 27 6:26 PM '49".
A red stamp at the very bottom reads "MAY 17 1949".
A handwritten signature appears next to the "G.I.R.-7" stamp.
A handwritten note in the right margin appears to be "JDL" or similar initials.
A distribution list is in the bottom left corner.

elements.description: Typed memorandum from SAC - San Antonio to Director - FBI dated April 26, 1949, regarding 'FLYING DISKS'.
Ink stamp reading 'RECORDED' with handwritten number '105' and red ink number '62-83894-168'.
Red ink stamp reading 'COMMUNICATIONS SECTION MAILED 9 APR 27 1949 P.M. FEDERAL BUREAU OF INVESTIGATION U. S. DEPARTMENT OF JUSTICE'.
Ink stamp reading 'RECEIVED READING ROOM APR 27 5 49 PM '49' and 'FBI U.S. DEPT OF JUSTICE'.
Vertical ink stamp reading 'FBI U.S. DEPT. OF JUSTICE' with date 'APR 27 6 26 PM '49'.
Small typed label in bottom left corner listing names (Mr. Tolson, Mr. Clegg, etc.).

judge.reason: Both descriptions agree on the presence and content of the same set of document stamps (e.g., 'COMMUNICATIONS SECTION MAILED', 'RECEIVED READING ROOM', 'G.I.R.-7', 'MAY 17 1949'), but Description B adds details (e.g., 'RECORDED' stamp with numbers, 'EX 10' stamp) that Description A omits without contradiction.
